data.title: Microservices
data.description: Amazon's architecture is based on a microservices model, allowing different parts of the application to be developed, deployed, and scaled independently.
data.technical_spec: Each service is a discrete piece of business functionality, with defined APIs.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon's infrastructure is designed to scale horizontally to handle large volumes of traffic and transactions.
data.technical_spec: AWS infrastructure, leveraging tools like load balancers, auto-scaling, and distributed databases.

data.title: Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA)
data.description: The architecture allows for integration of new services and features with minimal disruption.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled services communicated over standardized protocols.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: Event sourcing and distributed event management across systems improve updates and real-time processing.
data.technical_spec: Services capture and react to events using AWS SNS/SQS.

data.title: Global Network Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon operates on a global scale with data centers located worldwide to optimize content delivery.
data.technical_spec: AWS Global Accelerator and Edge caching via CloudFront.
